description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, angular form and a predominantly gray coloration. The surface is rough and uneven, featuring a mix of lighter and darker gray tones, with some subtle hints of brown and white, suggesting weathering and mineral deposits. The rock has a blocky appearance with several sharp edges and flat faces, as well as some rounded corners and natural indentations. Its texture appears coarse and granular, typical of sedimentary or metamorphic rocks found in urban landscaping. The overall mass and density suggest it is solid stone, likely used as a natural barrier or decorative element. There are no visible markings, graffiti, or signs of artificial modification on its surface.
description: A rugged, gray, blocky rock with rough, angular surfaces and subtle color variations, typical of urban landscaping or barrier use.
description_view_0: This view shows a rounded top with a prominent lighter patch and subtle layering on the upper surface.
description_view_1: This side reveals a flatter face with a darker, more textured surface and a slight overhang on the upper right.
description_view_2: This angle highlights a sloped, lighter face with visible striations and a more compact, vertical profile.
description_view_3: This view displays a stepped, layered structure with pronounced horizontal lines and a darker base.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
